one_liner: MU +1.1% to $1,145 — consolidating post-earnings gains (+17% on June 24 results); RSI 56 in neutral zone with near-BB-upper resistance at $1,234; demand exceeds supply beyond 2027 per management.

price_action.intraday_story: The wide intraday range ($1,023–$1,148) reflects the post-earnings volatility settling. Close at $1,145 near the high is constructive. The $1,023 low represents a significant intraday pullback of 9.3% that recovered — typical of a stock that gapped up sharply on earnings and is now finding its post-gap equilibrium.

news_catalyst.headline: Micron Q3 FY2026: Revenue, Margin, EPS All Beat; Data Center SSD >$5B, Doubled QoQ; Supply Tight Beyond 2027
news_catalyst.summary: Micron reported blowout Q3 results on June 24 with all three components (revenue, gross margin, EPS) above the high end of guidance. Data center SSD revenue exceeded $5B (more than doubled QoQ). Management extended the tight-supply outlook from 2026 to beyond calendar 2027. DRAM prices up 60% YoY. 16 multi-year strategic customer agreements (5-year terms through 2030) covering 20% of DRAM volume and one-third of NAND volume were announced, structurally reducing cyclicality. Apple price increases on consumer electronics the day after earnings highlighted Micron's upstream pricing power.

technical.summary: MU is post-earnings consolidation. RSI at 56 is neutral, MACD at +15.47 is firmly bullish. The stock sits below the upper BB ($1,234) with room to run. EMA50 ($1,047) and EMA200 ($1,037) are both well below current price — a clean uptrend. DCF at $1,192 shows modest 3.9% upside at current prices — not a deep value play at these levels but the earnings quality justifies the premium.

wyckoff_narrative: Post-earnings consolidation in neutral Wyckoff phase. The earnings gap-up was the SOS event; current price action is the post-SOS consolidation. If MU holds $1,023+ with declining volume over the next 1–2 weeks, that's the base for the next leg up toward $1,234 (BB upper). Any pullback to the $1,050–$1,100 zone on low volume would be the LPS entry.

valuation.summary: DCF at $1,192 reflects modest upside from current prices — MU's massive earnings beat is partially priced in. Grade A fundamentals (0.84 score) support the valuation. The strategic agreements reducing cyclicality are a qualitative positive that may justify a premium above DCF.

key_strengths: 60% DRAM price increases YoY — pricing power at historic highs
16 multi-year strategic agreements reduce historical cyclicality
Data center SSD revenue >$5B, doubled QoQ — explosive growth in highest-margin segment
key_concerns: Post-2028 demand moderation risk as data center builds complete
DCF shows only 3.9% upside — much of the earnings beat is priced in
Cyclical industry — multi-year agreements reduce but don't eliminate volatility
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with genuinely transformational earnings results. MU is priced for excellence but the structural supply-demand thesis extending beyond 2027 justifies the premium. Hold if positioned; new entry needs a pullback to the $1,050–$1,100 range.

risks: DCF only 3.9% upside at current price — valuation is full; a consensus miss would crater the stock
Post-2028 replacement cycle vs. new-build cycle shift could trigger the next downturn earlier than management guides
Apple component price increases flowing through are good for MU near-term but signal peak cycle pricing
